description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who must pull a small set of training artefacts from a “remote” storage area (already mounted on this workstation) into your local project workspace.

    1. Source location (already exists and is populated):  
       /home/user/remote_storage

       Its contents and structure are:

       • /home/user/remote_storage/images/dogs/dog1.jpg  
       • /home/user/remote_storage/images/dogs/dog2.jpg  
       • /home/user/remote_storage/images/cats/cat1.jpg  
       • /home/user/remote_storage/labels/dog_labels.csv  
       • /home/user/remote_storage/labels/cat_labels.csv  

       Each file is a tiny placeholder text file, **not** a real image; that is fine.

    2. Destination workspace that you must create:  
       /home/user/ml_project

       Inside that workspace create one subdirectory named data (i.e. /home/user/ml_project/data) where the synced files will live.

    3. Synchronisation requirements:  
       • Copy every file from the remote directory tree into /home/user/ml_project/data, preserving the exact directory structure shown above.  
       • Only these five files should appear in /home/user/ml_project/data (nothing extra).  
       • Use any standard Linux CLI tool you like (for example rsync, cp ‑r, or tar | tar). The solution should require no more than 3–4 shell commands.

    4. Logging requirement:  
       After the sync completes, create a plain-text log file at  
       /home/user/ml_project/sync_log.txt

       The file must contain **exactly five lines**, one per copied file, written in **alphabetical order** with paths **relative to /home/user/ml_project/data**.  
       The required format (each entry on its own line, no leading “./”, no blank lines, no extra whitespace):

       images/cats/cat1.jpg  
       images/dogs/dog1.jpg  
       images/dogs/dog2.jpg  
       labels/cat_labels.csv  
       labels/dog_labels.csv  

    5. Verification notes (what the automated grader will check):  
       • /home/user/ml_project exists.  
       • /home/user/ml_project/data contains the five files at the exact paths listed above.  
       • Every copied file’s byte-for-byte contents are identical to its counterpart in /home/user/remote_storage.  
       • /home/user/ml_project/sync_log.txt exists and contains exactly the five lines shown above, in that order, with a trailing newline after the final line.  
       • No additional files or directories are present inside /home/user/ml_project/data.

    Carry out the required shell commands to meet all criteria.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):

    Directory tree that already exists, with placeholder contents:

    /home/user/remote_storage/images/dogs/dog1.jpg
        → text content: "DOG1_PLACEHOLDER\n"
    /home/user/remote_storage/images/dogs/dog2.jpg
        → text content: "DOG2_PLACEHOLDER\n"
    /home/user/remote_storage/images/cats/cat1.jpg
        → text content: "CAT1_PLACEHOLDER\n"
    /home/user/remote_storage/labels/dog_labels.csv
        → text content: "id,label\n1,bulldog\n2,poodle\n"
    /home/user/remote_storage/labels/cat_labels.csv
        → text content: "id,label\n1,siamese\n2,maine_coon\n"

    Files/directories that must exist after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/ml_project  (directory)
    2. /home/user/ml_project/data  (directory)
    3. Inside /home/user/ml_project/data the exact copies of the five files listed above, with **identical byte contents**.
    4. /home/user/ml_project/sync_log.txt  (file)  
       Expected exact content (including final newline character):

images/cats/cat1.jpg
images/dogs/dog1.jpg
images/dogs/dog2.jpg
labels/cat_labels.csv
labels/dog_labels.csv
